slides.title: Asynchronous Programming ⏳
slides.content:
# Future and Async/Await

A `Future` represents a potential value or error that will be available at some time.

* Mark a function as `async` to use `await` inside.
* `await` pauses the function until the Future completes.
* Handle errors with try-catch.
* `FutureBuilder` widget helps integrate Futures into the UI.

quizData.question: What does the 'async' keyword do?

options.text: Makes a function run in a separate thread
Makes the function return a Future

quizData.explanation: async enables await and automatically wraps the return value in a Future.

slides.title: Async/Await Example
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Use async/await with try-catch

Always wrap await calls in try-catch to handle exceptions gracefully.
